name: Fior-Feis
shortDescription: Fior-Feis represents the 'true feast' or 'righteous celebration' in Celtic mythology, signifying a king's worthiness to rule and the prosperity of the land.
description: The Fior-Feis, often translated as 'true feast' or 'righteous celebration,' is a crucial concept within Celtic, particularly Irish, mythology relating to kingship and sovereignty. It represents a king's legitimacy and his ability to bring prosperity and abundance to his people. The king's worthiness was often tested through symbolic acts, rituals, or even magical challenges. A successful Fior-Feis indicated the king's rightful claim to the throne and his connection to the land. The concept is deeply intertwined with the idea of the sovereignty goddess, who bestows kingship upon a worthy leader. If the king was unjust or unfit, the Fior-Feis would be tainted, leading to famine, disease, and social unrest. The Lia Fáil's cry upon the rightful king's touch is a part of this concept of the king's confirmation by the land itself, a manifestation of the Fior-Feis. It is a reminder that the king's actions directly impact the wellbeing of his kingdom. The Fior-Feis is not simply a celebration; it is a validation of the king's rule and his harmonious relationship with the land and its people.

sources.title: The Celtic Myths: A Guide to the Ancient Myths and Legends
sources.author: Miranda J. Green
